entries.definition:
z-l-l to trip over, to slip, to cause to slip; to remove. Of this
root, three forms occur four times in the Qurʾan: زَلَّ zalla twice;
أَزَلَّ ʾazalla once and اِسْتَزَلَّ ʾistazalla once.

زَلَّ zalla i [v. intrans.] to trip, to slip (16:94) وَلَا تَتَّخِذُوا أَيْمَانَكُمْ دَخَلًا
بَيْنَكُمْ فَتَزِلَّ قَدَمٌ بَعْدَ ثُبُوتِهَا do not use your oaths as a means of deceit
between you, lest you lose your good standing within the
community/with God [lit. a foot should slip after being firmly
placed].

أَزَلَّ ʾazalla [v. IV, trans.] to cause to slip (off), to cause to fall,
to dislodge (2:36) فَأَزَلَّهُمَا الشَّيْطَانُ عَنْهَا فَأَخْرَجَهُمَا مِمَّا كَانَا فِيهِ but Satan
dislodged them from it [the Garden] and removed them from
what [the condition] they were in.

اِسْتَزَلَّ ʾistazalla [v. X, trans.] to cause to slip, to lead into
temptation (3:155) إِنَّمَا اسْتَزَلَّهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ بِبَعْضِ مَا كَسَبُوا in fact, Satan
made them slip by means of some of what they [themselves] had

403
